content_snippet: Received 6 DeepScientist-style citation-backed claims for citation hallucination verification.

Pneumonia Detection from Chest X-rays Using the CheXNet Deep Learning Algorithm - Early and accurate detection of pneumonia from chest X-ray images is crucial for timely treatment and patient care. In study presents a robust computational framework leveraging the CheXnet algorithm, a deep learning model, to identify pneumonia from chest X-rays. Developed a neural network architecture trained on a substantial dataset of chest X- ray images labeled as 'Normal' and 'Pneumonia'. The user- friendly interface of the system allows for seamless uploading of images, with the model subsequently providing predictions, a confusion matrix, accuracy metrics, and layer activations visualizations. The algorithm's performance was rigorously evaluated, with a confusion matrix indicating a high number of true positives and true negatives. Specifically, the model detected pneumonia with an accuracy of 92.47%, as shown by the results displayed in the system's interface. The precision, recall, and F1-score

Diagnostic performance of deep learning models versus radiologists in COVID-19 pneumonia: A systematic review and meta-analysis PURPOSE Although several studies have compared the performance of deep learning (DL) models and radiologists for the diagnosis of COVID-19 pneumonia on CT of the chest, these results have not been collectively evaluated. We performed a meta-analysis of original articles comparing the performance of DL models versus radiologists in detecting COVID-19 pneumonia. METHODS A systematic search was conducted on the three main medical literature databases, Scopus, Web of Science, and PubMed, for articles published as of February 1st, 2023. We included original scientific articles that compared DL models trained to detect COVID-19 pneumonia on CT to radiologists. Meta-analysis was performed to determine DL versus radiologist performance in terms of model sensitivity and specificity, taking into account inter and intra-study heterogeneity. ChronoXFormer: A Reproducible Longitudinal Vison Transformer Method for Pneumonia Progression from Chest X-rays This paper presents ChronoXFormer, a novel computer-aided diagnostic (CAD) framework designed to model pneumonia progression using longitudinal chest X-ray image sequences. Unlike conventional deep learning approaches that analyze radiographs independently, the proposed method explicitly incorporates temporal disease dynamics by leveraging consecutive follow-up images from the same patient.ChronoXFormer integrates a pretrained Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16) for spatial feature extraction with a temporal transformer encoder to capture disease evolution over time. The model jointly predicts pneumonia severity, disease stage (before, peak, after), and overall progression trends (improving, static, worsening), enabling patient-level monitoring from imaging data alone.The framework is evaluated on longitudinal cohorts derived from the CheXpert dataset, demonstrating strong performa

locator: Weakly Supervised Pneumonia Localization from Chest X-Rays Using Deep Neural Network and Grad-CAM Explanations
content_snippet: Weakly Supervised Pneumonia Localization from Chest X-Rays Using Deep Neural Network and Grad-CAM Explanations Chest X-ray imaging is commonly used to diagnose pneumonia, but accurately localizing the pneumonia affected regions typically requires detailed pixel-level annotations, which are costly and time consuming to obtain. To address this limitation, this study proposes a weakly supervised deep learning framework for pneumonia classification and localization using Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM). Instead of relying on costly pixel-level annotations, the proposed method utilizes image-level labels to generate clinically meaningful heatmaps that highlight pneumonia affected regions. Furthermore, we evaluate seven pre-trained deep learning models including a Vision Transformer under identical training conditions, using focal loss and patient-wise splits to prevent data leakage.
